personas.label: Care recipient
personas.details:
Physically/mentally healthy enough for aging or care at home.
60+ years old, 1 or more chronic conditions
Can benefit from the use of an intervention tool like Vlx
At risk for social isolation
living alone or with a spouse in a home environment
One or more paid professionals are involved in the individual's care
Some prior experience and comfort level with the use of technology
Limited need for physical assistance

personas.label: Family caregiver
personas.details: Adult child/spouse who has responsibility for aging parent and may still have children at home

personas.label: Care Coordinator
personas.details: A licensed or unlicensed professional who also has good technology, caregiving, and communications skills, and manages multiple care recipients

personas.label: Professional caregiver
personas.details: A licensed professional who gets paid to provide care for one or more care recipients and travels to the individual homes to provide care

personas.label: Self-Directed Care Recipient
personas.details: An individual aging senior that is responsible for directing their own care

personas.label: Agency or Association
personas.details: Responsible for structuring the care program and delivering value-based outcomes to a group of care recipients

personas.label: Segment Customer
personas.details:
An entity that offers the VLx App as a covered component of their offerings.

Disability Providers
Home Health Agencies
Elder care Law Firms
Strategic Partners
Senior Housing
Care Brokers
Care Coordination Orgs
PACE, LHCSA, CHHA
Government Entities - NYSOFA
Health Care Provider
Health Insurance (Payer)
Wealth Management Firms
Individual
Life Care Planning Law Firm
Employer Group

data.successCriteria:
Clients convert after trial 80%
Deliver trial value outcomes required by the agency
An in the market product that we can justify hiring a salesperson

features.name: Environmental Sensors Integration
features.note:
These are sensor-type devices that will detect things like carbon monoxide and other environmental concerns

Overlaps with many other features

features.name: NYSOFA Standards & Requirements
features.note: The NYS requirements and standards related to the delivery of applications, related infrastructure, use of AI, and other features and programmatic areas.

features.name: Daily Routine & ADL Tracking
features.note: We have components of this functionality, including tasks and reminders. We need to break the connections related to GrandCare dependencies

features.name: Clinical Trial Cohort Management
features.note: Part of a larger program and related data collection, analytics process

features.name: Health Education Content Library
features.note: Access to Trualta and other online resources

features.name: 911 & Emergency Services Integration
features.note: Calling out to 911 during an emergency from within the VLx App

features.name: Mobile App aka Family Caregiver App
features.note:
This is the primary set of features and functionality that runs on top of the VLx platform infrastructure.

This is primarily a catch all, unclear if it will continue to exist or be integrated into other features

features.name: In-App Feedback & NPS System
features.note:
The ability to gather User data while using the App to get feedback on features and how it is, or not meeting user needs.

What is the process of incorporating the NPS protocols into the deployment of the App

features.name: Longitudinal Care Timeline
features.note: Data collection defined in other cards, this is for the engine/algorithm

features.name: UI/UX Responsiveness and Device-Appropriateness
features.note:
Proper responsive design for the various viewport sizes of the individual device types and device families. WCAG/ADA should be a consideration and a complete UX/UI refresh.

Mobile App and Web Admin must be properly responsive for different devices.

UI/UX must account for ADA and other compliance requirements

Must have "Senior Mode" for large type, remove unnecessary clutter, etc.

features.name: Medication Tracking & Sharing
features.note: Need a detailed definition of Medication Tracking & Sharing

features.name: eCommerce - In-App Subscriptions
features.note: 1. eCommerce Integration; 2. In-app purchases (aka In-app subs); 3. Platform setup, Admin processes (e.g., subs price lists, etc.). Platform setup, administration of the related features.

features.name: Research Data Export & Open API
features.note: Engine/Alorithm (data collection covered in other cards)

features.name: Secure Document Vault
features.note: A PW-based system for access to documents (TBD third party, security within our existing doc mgt)
Foundational service for both security and business logging and metrics (related to Care Analysis/Metrics)
features.name: Platform Metrics/Logging

features.note: Since it's the first device integration, this effectively means developing an entirely new service/feature on the platform. Lots of testing and learning for initial device discovery/setup. These would be vitals or biometric readings that are not derived from MIA or the device or process used for facial scanning.
features.name: Connected Device Vitals - First Implementation
HIPAA Compatibility
features.note: Covers the actual personnel training and certification, the platform by definition should be up to spec

features.name: Voice AI Assistant
features.note: The ability to use Voice Interactive commands and capabilities within the App.

features.name: Role-Based Access Control + Audit Trail
features.note: Significant work has been done to specify RBAC and related security issues and areas of potential vulnerability. This should be under a feature area called "Security"

features.name: Care Analytics/Reporting
features.note: Foundational service in platform

features.name: Proprietary Wellness Score
features.note: This will touch everything and encompass a significant number of key areas across features as it will derive the data that drives the AI-enabled intelligence and algorithms that will define the proprietary scoring model.

features.name: To-Do List & Care Tasks
features.note: Specified updates and enhancements based on the next set of requirements in a phased development path. Need to determine what is most important at this stage from a calendaring perspective, and what other parts of the app need to be connected to this feature.
Foundatonal service in app
features.name: Health Risk Identification (Virtual Risk Assessment)
Caregiver Activity Logs (EVV)
features.note:
Required for verifying caregiver visit, and used for payment purposes. Medicare and Medicaid requirements.

TBD IF THIRD-PARTY SERVICE OR CUSTOM INTERNAL

features.name: EHR / Provider Integration
features.note: Where will this come into play, likely driven by the need for a group or healthcare provider, not in the immediate future? The relationship with the provider will drive requirements, specifications, and timeline.

features.name: Fall Detection
features.note:
Discovery for finding methods to determine when a user has a fall. Could be the integration of other devices and systems, like Cherish. Could be through acoustics like Sense.ai, camera-based technology, or wearables that provide fall detection capabilities.

Related to ER Avoidance & Acute Event Capture.

Also, look to what makes sense in terms of "Fall Prevention"

features.name: Shared Calendar
features.note: Specified updates and enhancements based on the next set of requirements in a phased development path. Need to determine what is most important at this stage from a calendaring perspective, and what other parts of the app need to be connected to this feature.

features.name: Senior Activity & Learning (GetSetUp)
features.note: This may be just simple links embedded in the UI? Needs further definition.

features.name: Offline Mode & Low-Bandwidth Support
features.note:
Connectivity status indicators should be established and ensure correctly defined and implemented. Offline-first logic will require analysis, work that must be estimated; we already indicate online status, though the UI needs to be clearer. Need to have a plan and schema/approach around defining offline capabilities.

Make sure to keep track of this feature and schedule it to be considered during the scoping of the MVP. It will be initiated as one of the first new features after MVP, and may be accelerated if a pilot or other programs require this functionality

features.name: Emergency Telemedicine Access
features.note: Connection to UCM-successor telemedicine app. Will need additional refinement, updates, or enhancements based on emerging specifications toward full definition.

features.name: Agentic AI Core (via Mia Health)
features.note:
A lot of this is driven by the extent to which MIA Health has matured in terms of capabilities. This may be a large undertaking, currently indeterminate.

Asumption is MIA will serve this functionality, but we must develop it agnostically of any partner.

features.name: ER Avoidance & Acute Event Capture
features.note:
Required as part of the NYS App requirements.

Part of the alert/messaging system. Pending a better definition of requirements, it's likely we have very little place to support this right now, and it will require more in-depth scoping to determine the level of effort required to support this feature.

features.name: Behavioral Nudge Engine
features.note:
This is Wellness guidance driven, which is based on AI algorithms that identify areas of concern for individuals and "nudges" them toward better behavior.

The engine/algorithm to manage collected data, industry guidelines, etc.

features.name: Facial Scan Vitals (via Mia Health)
features.note:
This is the NEW implementation of the integration with the MIA Health facial scanning process that quickly collects


Asumption is MIA will serve this functionality, but we must develop it agnostically of any partner.

features.name: Role-Based Vitals Reporting
features.note: This functionality refers to user roles and the permissions associated with what a user with specific access can see or do as it relates to vitals reporting.

features.name: Caregiver Support Resources
features.note: Trualta, etc. (service integration, sso?, without any functionality in-app)

features.name: HIPAA Compliance Certification
features.note: Engaging in a full sweep of the App and platform to address any areas where privacy is a concern, where restricted information may be exposed, including any updates or enhancements that support this effort.

features.name: Onboarding & Setup Flow
features.note: Onboarding must be easy, accessible, streamlined, and reduce friction across the onboarding process.

features.name: System Health Monitoring & Error Telemetry
features.note: The ability to capture data related to App uptime, system alerting, and performance reporting. Expand to include operation data

features.name: Content Sharing (Photos/Docs/Videos)
features.note:
Existing bare MVP functionality to share PDFs by uploading in Web Admin. Final size depends on final requirements.

Anyone in the family caregiving circle has permission to upload and download documents.

ABILITY TO ADD NEW FILES TYPES, AND MANAGE BY END USERS IN THE APP

features.name: AI Biometric Analytics
features.note: Foundational service and capabilities for collecting and analyzing data

features.name: Administrative Web Console
features.note:
Need to provide access for additional roles. Currently structured for high-level administration and the Life Care Coordinator level.

Any/all enhancements to make the UI iteself a professional UI.  It's not about adding features, it's about making existing and new features robust and user friendly.

features.name: Privacy-Forward Video (Consent-Only)
features.note: Hooks to be incorporated into the site, and upgrades and enhancements to support this and related features

features.name: Mia Health Base Integration
features.note: This card covers the foundation integration with MIA as a third party sevice.  Any actual use of the platform's features, oether than "Hello World" implementation, are covered by other cards

features.name: A/B Testing Infrastructure
features.note: Systematic approach to testing the various features, needs to apply resources to an effort like this.

features.name: Demo Account
features.note: Related to new users have ability to open and use app even without a subscription/care circle.  Related to new paradigm of making app easy to access.

features.name: Care Team Messaging
features.note: In-app circle members; Part of larger alerting/messaging system

features.name: Senior Social Connection
features.note: Need additional definition for this feature.

features.name: Video Conferencing
features.note:
Currently only works with GrandCare's platform, and is web based instead of in-app

We must implement the foundational service as well as workflows and other user/role based concerns.

features.name: SOC II Compliance
features.note:
Not needed until required by a business partner/customer

SOC II itself is an expensive certification, both initial and annual renewal.

Ideally, our platform and SDLC will alway meet major SOC II requirements, but there will likely be some amount of details and personel training.

features.name: Notification & Alert Management
features.note: There is a lot of API-driven functionality to update that will support receiving data and signals from 3rd-party services like the MIA Health facial scanning or related tools and services.

features.name: Medication Reminders
features.note: Need a detailed definition of Medication Reminders

features.name: Population (CR) Health Dashboard
features.note: The engine/algorithm ability to track groups of users metrics across user populations.  Overlaps with other data-collection cards.

features.name: Stress Detection
features.note: Engine/Algorithm on top of other cards about data collection

items.text: Solid client/caregiver onboarding flow with minimal support needed including subscription and billing (NEW?)
Confirm final success metrics and owners before go-live.
Data and analytics: Al to capture key data required by NYS and Vivalynx to provide insight and understanding
Regional issues, rural location, broadband access due to the availability in an area
Simple onboarding for low-tech users and caregivers.
A free-standing sellable product not designed just for trial but designed for sale
Mia Health AI integrated or similar in plan
Analytics - Security, Logging, History, Compliance
Initial rollout should prioritize lowest operational overhead.
Focus on VivaLynx's differentators in the marketplace
E-Commerce for in-app subscriptions
Design platform on our requirements, then fit-in vendors like MIA (lesson-learned from VLx requirements defined in part by key integration partners)
Which integrations are required for day-one reporting?
Analytics - Operational Data (use of features, user paths, utiization data, etc.)
Compliance
Analytics - Health/Medical Outcomes, other health/medical data
Which outcomes are mandatory for MVP success sign-off?
Ability to demo as we develop
MVP scope and timeline must fit implementation windows.
Data quality and missing inputs could reduce confidence in MVP analytics.
Vitals and biometrics gathering (such as MIA)
Native App on App Store/Google Play Store in Plan
